data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: WhatsApp employs a microservices architecture for scalability and flexibility in managing different functionalities independently.
data.technical_spec: Individual services communicate over RESTful APIs with load balancers for traffic management.

data.title: Client-Server Model
data.description: The app uses a classic client-server model where user requests are processed on remote servers.
data.technical_spec: Client requests are routed through secure and distributed servers before reaching end recipients.

data.title: End-to-End Encryption Protocol
data.description: WhatsApp uses Signal Protocol for end-to-end encryption of messages, ensuring privacy and security.
data.technical_spec: Data transmitted between users is encrypted with cryptographic keys known only to the communicating clients.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: The system uses load balancing to distribute incoming network traffic across multiple servers.
data.technical_spec: Load balancers ensure no single server becomes overwhelmed with too many requests.

data.title: Global Distribution via CDNs
data.description: Content delivery networks (CDNs) are used for fast, efficient global data distribution.
data.technical_spec: Static and dynamic content is cached and delivered from geographically dispersed data centers.
